title: Prayer
content:
We doubt the word that tells us: Ask,
            And ye shall have your prayer;
        We turn our thoughts as to a task,
            With will constrained and rare.

        And yet we have; these scanty prayers
            Yield gold without alloy:
        O God, but he that trusts and dares
            Must have a boundless joy!
meaning:
This evocative piece by George MacDonald, titled "Prayer", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
